articles.headline: Intune Community Highlights Agents, Windows 365 Reserve, and Cloud PKI
articles.summary: This two-week Intune roundup covers vulnerability-remediation agents, Security Copilot capabilities, managed VS Code extensions, Windows 365 changes, and Cloud PKI. Endpoint administrators can use the collection to identify recent features and community guidance worth evaluating.

articles.headline: Entra ID adds granular authentication claims to SAML applications
articles.summary: Entra administrators can expose detailed authentication-method information to SAML applications by configuring the optional amr claim with include_granular_amr. This enables applications to evaluate phishing-resistant MFA, but administrators must configure most SAML apps manually.

articles.headline: Microsoft Publishes XFS Data-Fork Mapping Vulnerability
articles.summary: Microsoft published CVE-2026-64600 concerning XFS data-fork mapping after the filesystem cycles ILOCK. Administrators running affected Linux systems should assess exposure and install applicable kernel or vendor updates.

articles.headline: Microsoft Discloses Process-Kill Vulnerability in seunshare killall()
articles.summary: Microsoft published CVE-2026-59677, which describes a process-kill attack vector in the seunshare killall() function. Security administrators should review affected systems and apply the relevant vendor updates or mitigations.

articles.headline: Microsoft discloses local file deletion flaw in seunshare rm_rf()
articles.summary: Microsoft published information about CVE-2026-59676, a local file deletion vulnerability in the rm_rf() function used by seunshare. Security teams should review the advisory, identify affected systems, and apply available mitigations or updates.

articles.headline: Teams PowerShell 7.9 adopts WAM and adds federated chat controls
articles.summary: Microsoft Teams PowerShell 7.9 now uses Windows Web Account Manager for authentication and expands controls for external federation. Administrators should test automation and module compatibility because WAM can be disabled only temporarily and existing assembly conflicts remain.

articles.headline: Microsoft Entra lets passwordless users change passwords without SSPR
articles.summary: Starting in October 2026, passwordless users will be able to change their password in My Sign-Ins by authenticating with a passkey or Windows Hello. Identity administrators should review help-desk procedures and access policies because the workflow will no longer depend on Self-Service Password Reset.

articles.headline: SharePoint Adds Viva Amplify Campaign Management and Analytics
articles.summary: Microsoft is bringing Viva Amplify campaign creation, distribution, and analytics directly into SharePoint in August 2026. Administrators should review licensing and governance because the capability initially applies only to Amplify users.

articles.headline: Defender for Office 365 blocks prompt injection attacks against Copilot
articles.summary: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 2 can classify messages containing prompt injections as high-confidence phishing and block them from user mailboxes. Administrators can reduce the risk that malicious email content manipulates Microsoft 365 Copilot into exposing sensitive data.

articles.headline: Microsoft Entra expands identity management integrations for SAP
articles.summary: Microsoft Entra now supports more flexible SAP provisioning, custom user attributes, and account discovery. The integrations help administrators replace SAP IDM while automating identity lifecycles and strengthening access governance.

articles.headline: Microsoft shares lessons from scaling a Copilot connector to one million documents
articles.summary: Microsoft detailed the architecture, governance, schema, and retrieval practices behind a production Microsoft 365 Copilot connector serving more than 300 agents. Platform teams can use these lessons to improve secure knowledge grounding and retrieval quality at enterprise scale.

articles.headline: Microsoft 365 Copilot expands the enterprise data risk surface
articles.summary: The analysis explains how Copilot can rapidly discover and combine Microsoft 365 data that users already have permission to access. Administrators should review oversharing, identity controls, data governance, and Zero Trust readiness before expanding deployments.

articles.headline: Teams voice phishing attempts surge as Tycoon2FA email attacks decline
articles.summary: Microsoft reports that Tycoon2FA-linked phishing volume fell 92% after its disruption operation, while malicious Teams calls rose to nearly ten times the mid-2025 baseline. Security teams should adjust detection and training as attackers shift from email toward trusted collaboration channels.

articles.headline: Azure AI Search SSRF Flaw Enables Privilege Escalation
articles.summary: An SSRF vulnerability in Azure AI Search allows an authenticated attacker to elevate privileges remotely. Administrators should apply Microsoft's remediation and review access to affected search services.

articles.headline: AKS Authentication Flaw Allows Remote Privilege Escalation
articles.summary: A missing authentication check in Azure Kubernetes Service allows an unauthenticated attacker to elevate privileges remotely. AKS administrators should prioritize remediation and inspect exposed clusters for suspicious activity.

articles.headline: Microsoft Account Buffer Overflow Enables Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: A heap-based buffer overflow in Microsoft Account allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute code remotely. Administrators should prioritize Microsoft's remediation and monitor account infrastructure for signs of exploitation.

articles.headline: Microsoft Surface Input Validation Flaw Enables Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: Improper input validation in Microsoft Surface allows an authenticated attacker to execute code remotely. Endpoint administrators should deploy Microsoft's remediation and identify affected Surface devices in their fleet.

articles.headline: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Flaw Enables Remote Privilege Escalation
articles.summary: Improper authorization in Azure Red Hat OpenShift allows an authenticated attacker to elevate privileges remotely. Administrators should remediate affected clusters and review privileged activity for evidence of abuse.

articles.headline: Azure API Management Flaw Enables Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: CVE-2026-35425 lets an authorized network attacker exploit improper access controls in Azure API Management to execute code. Administrators should review affected APIM deployments, restrict privileged access, and apply Microsoft’s recommended mitigations or updates.

articles.headline: Microsoft Graph Flaw Exposes Sensitive Information
articles.summary: CVE-2026-49159 lets an authorized network attacker disclose sensitive information through Microsoft Graph. Administrators should assess affected applications, review Graph permissions, and apply Microsoft’s recommended mitigations or updates.

articles.headline: Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Enables Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: CVE-2026-50517 lets an authorized network attacker execute code by exploiting unsafe deserialization in Microsoft 365 Copilot. Administrators should review Copilot access, monitor suspicious activity, and apply Microsoft’s recommended mitigations or updates.

articles.headline: Exchange Online Authentication Flaw Enables Remote Tampering
articles.summary: CVE-2026-56191 lets an unauthenticated network attacker tamper with Microsoft Exchange Online through improper authentication. Administrators should review Exchange audit activity and follow Microsoft’s recommended mitigations or service guidance.

articles.headline: Data Quality SSRF Flaw Enables Privilege Escalation
articles.summary: CVE-2026-57106 lets an unauthenticated network attacker exploit server-side request forgery in Data Quality to elevate privileges. Administrators should limit service exposure, monitor outbound requests, and apply Microsoft’s recommended mitigations or updates.

articles.headline: Azure Key Vault Authentication Flaw Enables Privilege Escalation
articles.summary: CVE-2026-62825 lets an unauthenticated network attacker exploit improper authentication in Azure Key Vault to elevate privileges. Administrators should review vault access logs and policies and apply Microsoft’s recommended mitigations or updates.

articles.headline: Azure App Service flaw enables privilege escalation on Azure Stack Hub
articles.summary: Microsoft disclosed an improper access control vulnerability in Azure App Service on Azure Stack Hub. An unauthorized network attacker could elevate privileges, so administrators should review affected deployments and apply Microsoft's remediation promptly.

articles.headline: Azure DNS authorization flaw allows privilege escalation
articles.summary: Microsoft disclosed a missing authorization vulnerability in Azure DNS that permits privilege escalation over a network. Azure administrators should identify affected resources and prioritize Microsoft's recommended remediation.

articles.headline: Microsoft Online Services flaw exposes information over the network
articles.summary: Microsoft disclosed an improper authorization vulnerability affecting its online services. An unauthorized network attacker could access information, so administrators should assess exposure and follow Microsoft's mitigation guidance.

articles.headline: Compare Entra Tenant Governance with Azure Lighthouse delegation
articles.summary: The article compares Microsoft Entra Tenant Governance with Azure Lighthouse and explains how their delegation models operate across different boundaries. Administrators and service providers can use the guidance to choose between tenant-level governance and Azure resource management without creating unnecessary identities.

articles.headline: Windows 11 ISO Files Keep Growing Across Hudson Valley Releases
articles.summary: Windows 11 installation media continues to grow across successive Hudson Valley releases. IT administrators should account for the larger files when planning deployment storage, network capacity, and provisioning workflows.

articles.headline: DNS fix blocks ghost domain renewal through stale glue records
articles.summary: An additional fix for CVE-2026-40622 clamps the TTL of A and AAAA records to prevent one-time ghost domain delegation renewal through glue records. Administrators should deploy the updated remediation because the original fix did not fully close the issue.

articles.headline: Wildcard replay flaw enables DNS cache poisoning in expired replies
articles.summary: A wildcard replayed as different data can poison responses generated through the serve-expired path. Administrators running affected DNS services should patch promptly and monitor for unexpected cached records or resolution behavior.

articles.headline: DNS-over-QUIC flaw lets attackers bypass limits and cause denial of service
articles.summary: A flaw in DNS-over-QUIC handling allows remote attackers to bypass the quic-size budget and exhaust resources. Administrators should review affected DNS deployments and apply the relevant security update to prevent service disruption.

articles.headline: Malformed DNSCrypt TCP packet can crash Unbound
articles.summary: CVE-2026-40691 is a packet-of-death vulnerability affecting DNSCrypt over TCP. Administrators running exposed DNSCrypt services should prioritize assessment because a malicious packet could remotely interrupt DNS availability.

articles.headline: Unbound CNAME redirect handling can crash DNS resolvers
articles.summary: CVE-2026-55717 can crash Unbound when serve-expired-client-timeout interacts with a response-ip CNAME redirect. Administrators using these features should assess exposure because crafted or unexpected DNS responses could interrupt name resolution.

articles.headline: Unbound reply-threshold setting can terminate applications
articles.summary: CVE-2026-44621 can abruptly terminate applications using libunbound when unwanted-reply-threshold is configured. Administrators should identify affected resolver integrations because repeated termination could disrupt DNS-dependent services.

articles.headline: Unbound control command can expose local data across views
articles.summary: CVE-2026-55708 is a privacy and configuration flaw triggered when administrators add local data to views through unbound-control. Teams using split DNS or view-specific records should investigate because data may not remain isolated as intended.

articles.headline: Unbound accepts invalid primary hostnames for zone transfers
articles.summary: CVE-2026-50248 allows Unbound to accept a BOGUS-configured primary hostname for zone transfers in authoritative and response-policy zones. DNS administrators should review affected configurations because unintended transfer behavior can undermine zone integrity and policy enforcement.

articles.headline: Unbound NXDOMAIN flaw can shadow legitimate DNS zones
articles.summary: CVE-2026-44687 is an off-by-one error in Unbound's harden-below-nxdomain logic that can let a legitimate parent NXDOMAIN shadow a stub or forward zone. Administrators should assess affected DNS resolver configurations because the flaw can disrupt access to delegated resources.

articles.headline: DNS Error Reporting Option Can Cause a Stack Buffer Overflow
articles.summary: CVE-2026-55973 links the enabled dns-error-reporting option to a stack buffer overflow. Administrators should locate affected software, review whether the option is enabled, and follow vendor mitigation or update guidance.

articles.headline: GNU diffutils Buffer Overflow Threatens Systems Using the Package
articles.summary: CVE-2026-53910 is a heap-based buffer overflow in GNU diffutils. Administrators should inventory Linux workloads and development environments that include affected versions and apply distribution updates when available.

articles.headline: Elasticsearch Resource Consumption Flaw Can Disrupt Availability
articles.summary: CVE-2026-63136 involves uncontrolled resource consumption in Elasticsearch and can cause denial of service. Administrators should review affected versions and prioritize vendor-recommended mitigations for exposed deployments.

articles.headline: Reachable Assertion Can Crash Elasticsearch Services
articles.summary: CVE-2026-63140 exposes a reachable assertion in Elasticsearch that can lead to denial of service. Administrators should identify affected deployments and follow Elasticsearch remediation guidance to protect service availability.

articles.headline: Elasticsearch Resource Exhaustion Can Trigger Denial of Service
articles.summary: CVE-2026-56145 allows uncontrolled resource consumption in Elasticsearch, potentially causing denial of service. Administrators running Elasticsearch in Microsoft-connected environments should assess exposure and apply vendor guidance when available.

articles.headline: Permission Creep Can Block Microsoft Graph PowerShell Sessions
articles.summary: Accumulated delegated permissions on the Microsoft Graph Command Line Tools service principal can cause scope problems during interactive PowerShell sessions. Entra administrators should review existing consent grants and request only the scopes required for each task.

articles.headline: SharePoint News gains cross-posting to Viva Engage
articles.summary: SharePoint News authors will be able to distribute posts to Viva Engage while preserving their formatting. A shared Engage conversation will replace separate comments, giving administrators a consistent discussion thread across Microsoft 365 surfaces.

articles.headline: Classic Outlook adds the unified Microsoft 365 Copilot entry point
articles.summary: Microsoft is bringing its unified Copilot entry point to classic Outlook in August 2026. The interface consolidates contextual Copilot actions across Microsoft 365, so administrators should prepare users and support materials for the rollout.

articles.headline: Microsoft and AXA XL coordinate incident response for cyber insurance customers
articles.summary: Microsoft and AXA XL are integrating Defender Experts incident response services into cyber insurance workflows. The partnership aims to help security, legal, executive, and insurance teams coordinate faster during incidents such as ransomware attacks.

articles.headline: Microsoft Adds TPM Attestation to Secure Windows KMS Activation
articles.summary: Microsoft is introducing KMS Hardware-Secured, which uses TPM-based attestation to verify that activation hosts run on trusted hardware. Administrators should prepare upcoming Windows Server KMS deployments for hardware-backed validation to reduce spoofing, credential theft, and licensing risk.

articles.headline: Intune Adds Automatic Updates for Managed macOS PKG Apps
articles.summary: Intune service release 2606 lets administrators automatically update macOS PKG applications by updating their app policies. This reduces manual packaging work and helps managed Macs receive current application versions more consistently.

articles.headline: 12 Free Tools Simplify Microsoft 365 Administration and Security
articles.summary: AdminDroid highlights 12 free tools for Microsoft 365 tasks such as configuration backup, drift detection, Conditional Access deployment, security assessment, and reporting. Administrators can use the list to fill gaps in native admin centers, but should review each community tool before granting tenant access.

articles.headline: Microsoft Publishes CVE-2026-56434 for the NGINX SSI Module
articles.summary: Microsoft has published an advisory for CVE-2026-56434 affecting the NGINX ngx_http_ssi_module. Administrators using server-side includes should check affected versions and follow the available remediation guidance.

articles.headline: Microsoft Publishes CVE-2026-42533 for NGINX Map and Regex Matching
articles.summary: Microsoft has published an advisory for CVE-2026-42533 involving NGINX map directives and regular-expression matching. Administrators running NGINX should review the advisory, identify affected versions, and apply vendor guidance.

articles.headline: RabbitMQ Management UI on Windows Is Vulnerable to UNC SSRF
articles.summary: Microsoft disclosed CVE-2026-57211, a UNC-based server-side request forgery vulnerability affecting the RabbitMQ management UI on Windows. Administrators should review the advisory, assess affected deployments, and restrict management-interface exposure while following available remediation guidance.

articles.headline: RabbitMQ Fixes Remote Guest Sessions Caused by Loopback Checks
articles.summary: Faulty listener-address checks could treat remote AMQP and Stream Protocol connections as loopback traffic, enabling guest sessions remotely. Administrators should patch RabbitMQ immediately and verify that guest access remains restricted to localhost.

articles.headline: RabbitMQ Fixes Stored XSS in Federation Management Plugin
articles.summary: The RabbitMQ federation management plugin rendered unsanitized consumer tags, enabling stored cross-site scripting. Administrators should patch affected management interfaces and restrict console access until updates are deployed.

articles.headline: RabbitMQ Fixes Cross-Tenant Topic Authorization Bypass
articles.summary: A RabbitMQ topic-authorization flaw could let messages bypass routing-key restrictions across tenant boundaries. Administrators running shared or multi-tenant brokers should update promptly and review topic permissions.

articles.headline: RabbitMQ Fixes Unauthenticated Memory-Exhaustion Attack
articles.summary: RabbitMQ's Stream listener failed to enforce its configured frame-size limit during authentication, allowing an unauthenticated client to exhaust memory. Administrators should patch exposed brokers promptly to prevent remote denial-of-service attacks.

articles.headline: Qualcomm RMNET Fixes Use-After-Free in Endpoint Removal
articles.summary: The Qualcomm RMNET driver contained a use-after-free flaw when removing a network endpoint. Administrators should update affected Linux systems to reduce the risk of crashes or memory corruption.

articles.headline: Linux ipset Fixes Race Condition During List Resizing
articles.summary: A race condition between ipset dumps and list resizing could cause unsafe behavior in the Linux netfilter subsystem. Administrators should apply the relevant security updates to systems that use ipset-based traffic filtering.

articles.headline: Microsoft Publishes CVE-2026-64192 for Linux BPF Inode Storage
articles.summary: Microsoft has published an advisory for CVE-2026-64192 involving BPF inode-storage map creation when BPF LSM is uninitialized. Administrators responsible for Linux systems should review kernel exposure and apply applicable updates.

articles.headline: Microsoft Publishes CVE-2026-26197 for HDF5 Array Validation
articles.summary: Microsoft has published an advisory for CVE-2026-26197 concerning inconsistent array size validation in H5Odtype.c. Administrators should identify software that bundles the affected HDF5 code and follow vendor remediation guidance.

articles.headline: Purview DLP Preview Blocks File Sharing with Named Domains and Users
articles.summary: Microsoft Purview DLP can now block external sharing of SharePoint and OneDrive files with specified domains or individual users in preview. Administrators can use targeted allow or deny lists to reduce data leakage without blocking all external collaboration.

articles.headline: Entra ID Retires Custom CSS Positioning for Company Branding
articles.summary: Microsoft will retire custom CSS positioning properties from Entra ID company branding on October 26, 2026. Administrators should review affected sign-in experiences and replace unsupported styling before layouts change.

articles.headline: Microsoft Purview extends DLP controls to non-Microsoft apps
articles.summary: Microsoft Purview is extending Data Loss Prevention and automatic sensitivity labeling to connected services including Google Workspace, Box, Dropbox, Salesforce, AWS, and Webex. Security teams should review application-specific conditions and actions before the preview in July 2026 and general availability in September 2026.

articles.headline: Windows 11 enforces smart card removal in Entra-authenticated remote sessions
articles.summary: Windows 11 Insider Build 28120.2546 lets administrators automatically disconnect Azure Virtual Desktop and Windows 365 sessions when a redirected smart card is removed. The change extends smart card removal policies to Entra-authenticated remote sessions, helping organizations enforce security and compliance requirements.

articles.headline: Dynamics 365 Sales agents connect to external systems through MCP
articles.summary: Microsoft described how Dynamics 365 Sales agents built with Copilot Studio can access external tools and trusted data through Model Context Protocol. Administrators and developers can reduce custom integration work, but must govern which systems and actions agents can access.

articles.headline: Microsoft Entra retires custom CSS positioning for branded sign-ins
articles.summary: Microsoft Entra ID will retire custom CSS positioning properties for branded sign-in pages globally on October 26, 2026, followed by all custom CSS later in 2027. Administrators should identify affected tenants and update branding to avoid broken layouts while improving phishing resistance.

articles.headline: Evaluate third-party patching tools beyond licensing costs
articles.summary: This analysis recommends assessing third-party patching products by operational value rather than licensing price alone. Intune administrators should compare deployment reliability, application coverage, automation, reporting, and support before selecting a platform.

articles.headline: Microsoft updates credit for Windows NTFS remote code execution flaw
articles.summary: Microsoft updated an acknowledgement for CVE-2026-58640 without changing the vulnerability details or remediation guidance. Administrators do not need to take additional action beyond applying the existing security update.

articles.headline: Microsoft updates credit for Windows WinSock privilege escalation flaw
articles.summary: Microsoft updated an acknowledgement for CVE-2026-50462 without changing the vulnerability details or remediation guidance. Administrators do not need to take additional action beyond applying the existing security update.

articles.headline: Open-source tool locks devices when users remove their YubiKey
articles.summary: Sciber YubiKey Locker can lock a device or sign out the user when a YubiKey is removed on Windows, macOS, or Linux. IT administrators can deploy and configure the tool through Intune and ADMX policies to enforce security-key removal behavior.

articles.headline: Conditional Access replaces static remembered MFA sessions
articles.summary: Conditional Access sign-in frequency provides more granular reauthentication controls than the tenant-wide Remember MFA setting. Entra administrators can apply policies by user, application, and risk context instead of relying on a fixed browser cookie.

articles.headline: AI skills streamline development of Maester security tests
articles.summary: Reusable instruction files help AI agents generate Maester tests that follow the project's expected structure and workflow. Entra and Microsoft 365 administrators can turn security-control ideas into reviewable tests without first mastering the entire codebase.

articles.headline: Linux OCFS2 flaw accepts oversized group bitmap descriptors
articles.summary: The Linux OCFS2 filesystem requires stricter validation to reject oversized group bitmap descriptors. The issue has limited relevance to Microsoft administrators unless affected Linux workloads use OCFS2 in their environment.

articles.headline: QEMU Hyper-V debugging flaw enables an out-of-bounds host write
articles.summary: A missing mapped-length check in QEMU-KVM's Hyper-V synthetic debugger can cause an out-of-bounds write on the host. Administrators operating affected virtualization hosts should assess exposure and apply vendor fixes when available.

articles.headline: BusyBox heap overflow allows crafted input to trigger denial of service
articles.summary: A heap overflow in the BusyBox ash shell can let crafted input cause a denial of service. Administrators running affected BusyBox-based containers or appliances should review vendor guidance and update exposed workloads.

articles.headline: AppArmor Adds Controls for Implicit TCP Fast Open Connections
articles.summary: AppArmor now mediates implicit connections created when applications use sendmsg with TCP Fast Open. Linux administrators should update affected systems to ensure security policies govern this connection path.

articles.headline: Linux TIPC Fix Prevents Use-After-Free During AEAD Decryption
articles.summary: A Linux kernel fix addresses a slab use-after-free read in the TIPC AEAD decryption callback. Administrators running Linux systems with TIPC enabled should review affected versions and apply the relevant security update.

articles.headline: AMD GPU driver fixes NULL pointer flaw in KFD shared virtual memory
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update fixes a NULL pointer bug in the AMD KFD shared virtual memory implementation. Administrators running Linux systems with AMD GPUs should assess exposure and apply applicable vendor updates.

articles.headline: AMD GPU driver fixes page retrieval flaw in amdgpu HMM
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update fixes a flaw in the AMD GPU driver's amdgpu_hmm_range_get_pages function. Administrators running Linux systems with AMD GPUs should review the CVE and apply applicable vendor updates.

articles.headline: Linux Netfilter Uses Two-Stage Removal to Protect ebtables Objects
articles.summary: Linux Netfilter now removes ebtables objects in two stages to prevent unsafe lifecycle handling. Administrators running Linux bridges or container networking should apply the update after confirming their kernel is affected.

articles.headline: KVM SEV Fix Rejects Zero-Length Port I/O Requests
articles.summary: KVM now ignores zero-length port I/O requests when running AMD SEV-protected virtual machines. Virtualization administrators should update affected Linux hosts to avoid incorrect request handling at the guest-host boundary.

articles.headline: AMD Display Driver Validates GPIO Tables to Prevent Invalid Access
articles.summary: The Linux AMD display driver now validates the GPIO pin lookup table size before iterating over it. Administrators managing affected Linux endpoints should deploy the fix to prevent invalid memory access caused by malformed table data.

articles.headline: Linux ALSA Fix Blocks Out-of-Bounds Access in ASI HPI Cache
articles.summary: A Linux ALSA update fixes a potential out-of-bounds array access while the ASI HPI driver reads its cache. Administrators using affected audio hardware should apply the update to reduce the risk of crashes or unintended memory access.

articles.headline: Microsoft Graph batching accelerates large-scale Microsoft 365 automation
articles.summary: Microsoft Graph can process up to 20 requests in one JSON batch, reducing network calls for bulk queries and updates. Administrators can use batching to accelerate user, group, device, license, and mailbox automation in large tenants.

articles.headline: OneNote brings multimodal Copilot Notebook capture to Android
articles.summary: OneNote for Android will let users combine audio transcription, images, and typed notes in one capture session before Copilot creates a structured page. The feature enters preview in August 2026 and general availability in September, giving administrators another Copilot data workflow to evaluate and govern.

articles.headline: Copilot Studio adds run-only sharing for autonomous agents
articles.summary: Copilot Studio will allow makers to share autonomous agents with end users without granting edit permissions or ownership. The preview starts in August 2026, helping administrators broaden agent access while retaining governance and separation between builders and users.

articles.headline: Microsoft 365 Copilot adds grounded answers from Power BI data
articles.summary: Microsoft 365 Copilot can now reason over Power BI reports and semantic models to answer natural-language questions with enterprise data. The integration reaches general availability in August 2026, requiring administrators to review Power BI permissions and Copilot data-governance controls.

articles.headline: SharePoint improves flexible page section authoring
articles.summary: SharePoint is adding persistent layout guides, clearer column controls, and one-click conversion of existing sections into Flexible Sections. The August 2026 release should simplify page authoring across desktop, mobile, and email experiences but has limited administrative impact.

articles.headline: Microsoft Unifies App and Agent Installation Across Microsoft 365
articles.summary: Microsoft will unify app and agent installation changes across Teams, Outlook, and Microsoft 365 Copilot in August 2026. Administrators will be able to configure installations once and enforce them consistently across supported surfaces.

articles.headline: Microsoft previews stronger Zero Trust controls for AI and private access
articles.summary: Microsoft is previewing new Entra Internet Access and Private Access controls for AI interactions, sensitive data, applications, and connectivity resilience. Administrators can apply identity, device, location, and risk context across more traffic, including access initiated by AI agents.

articles.headline: Microsoft Releases New Windows 11 Preview Builds Across Three Channels
articles.summary: Microsoft released Windows 11 preview builds for Beta, Experimental, and Release Preview channels, covering versions from 24H2 through 26H1. Endpoint administrators should review the channel-specific notes before validating upcoming Windows changes in managed test rings.

articles.headline: Microsoft releases Azure AD B2C Migration Policy Analyzer
articles.summary: Microsoft has made the Migration Policy Analyzer generally available for assessing Azure AD B2C custom policies before moving to Entra External ID. Identity teams can inventory authentication capabilities, identify migration gaps, and reduce manual analysis, although the tool does not scan the entire tenant.

articles.headline: Company Portal App Loading Failures Trace Back to IWService Errors
articles.summary: The troubleshooting analysis connects Company Portal app-loading failures with matching web portal errors and HTTP 500 or 503 responses from IWService. Intune administrators can use these signals to distinguish service-side failures from device-specific Company Portal problems.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates Product Details for Entra ID CVE-2026-50652
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the Entra ID denial-of-service vulnerability CVE-2026-50652. The revision is informational and does not indicate a new vulnerability or security update.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates Product Details for Entra ID CVE-2026-50653
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the Entra ID denial-of-service vulnerability CVE-2026-50653. The revision is informational and does not introduce a new vulnerability or security update.

articles.headline: Microsoft Revises Product Details for .NET CVE-2026-47304
articles.summary: Microsoft revised the product information for the .NET security feature bypass vulnerability CVE-2026-47304. Administrators should note the clarified applicability, but the change is informational and does not provide a new update.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates ADFS Product Details for CVE-2026-50304
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the ADFS denial-of-service vulnerability CVE-2026-50304. The revision is informational and does not indicate a new vulnerability or security update.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates ADFS Product Details for CVE-2026-50368
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the ADFS denial-of-service vulnerability CVE-2026-50368. The revision is informational and does not indicate a new vulnerability or security update.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates ADFS Product Data for CVE-2026-50324
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected product information for this ADFS denial-of-service vulnerability. The change is informational only, so administrators do not need to reassess remediation based on this update alone.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates ADFS Product Data for CVE-2026-50355
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected product information for this ADFS denial-of-service vulnerability. The change is informational only, so administrators do not need to reassess remediation based on this update alone.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates ADFS Product Data for CVE-2026-50411
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected product information for this ADFS denial-of-service vulnerability. The change is informational only, so administrators do not need to reassess remediation based on this update alone.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates .NET Product Data for CVE-2026-50525
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected product information for this .NET denial-of-service vulnerability. The change is informational only, so administrators do not need to reassess remediation based on this update alone.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates .NET Framework Product Data for CVE-2026-50527
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected product information for this .NET Framework denial-of-service vulnerability. The change is informational only, so administrators do not need to reassess remediation based on this update alone.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates .NET Framework Product Data for CVE-2026-50646
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected product information for this .NET Framework remote-code-execution vulnerability. The change is informational only, so administrators do not need to reassess remediation based on this update alone.

articles.headline: Microsoft Revises ADFS CVE-2026-50647 Product Information
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the ADFS denial-of-service vulnerability. The change is informational only, but administrators should verify their patch inventories against the revised table.

articles.headline: Microsoft Revises .NET Framework CVE-2026-50648 Product Information
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the .NET Framework denial-of-service vulnerability. No new security guidance was announced, but administrators should reconcile the revised table with their patch scope.

articles.headline: Microsoft Revises .NET CVE-2026-50649 Product Information
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the affected-product information for the .NET remote-code-execution vulnerability. The revision is informational, but administrators should confirm that patching reports include every listed product.

articles.headline: Microsoft Updates Product Details for CVE-2026-50650
articles.summary: Microsoft revised the affected product information for the .NET Framework elevation-of-privilege vulnerability. The change is informational and does not introduce new security guidance or require additional administrator action.

articles.headline: Microsoft Revises Build Numbers for Business Central CVE-2024-35248
articles.summary: Microsoft corrected build numbers associated with the Dynamics 365 Business Central elevation-of-privilege vulnerability. Administrators can use the revised values to verify deployments, but the update adds no new remediation guidance.

articles.headline: Azure Lighthouse Limits Partner Identity Footprints in Customer Tenants
articles.summary: The analysis explains how Azure Lighthouse lets service providers manage Azure resources without creating guest or local accounts in customer tenants. Administrators can reduce identity cleanup, persistent-access risk, and credential-management overhead while retaining delegated control.

articles.headline: Linux batman-adv limits unacknowledged throughput-meter entries
articles.summary: CVE-2026-63834 restricts the number of unacknowledged entries maintained by the Linux batman-adv throughput meter. Administrators using mesh-networking features should review affected kernels and install the relevant security update.

articles.headline: Linux HDLC PPP synchronizes timers before freeing state
articles.summary: CVE-2026-63803 fixes Linux HDLC PPP timer synchronization before the driver frees its state. Administrators using affected Linux networking components should evaluate exposure and apply the relevant security update.

articles.headline: Linux BPF frees replaced sysctl write buffers safely
articles.summary: CVE-2026-63809 changes Linux BPF code to use kvfree() for a replaced sysctl write buffer. Administrators should review affected Linux systems and deploy the relevant security update to reduce memory-handling risk.

articles.headline: Linux nfsd resets write verifier after deferred writeback errors
articles.summary: CVE-2026-53393 addresses how Linux nfsd handles the write verifier after deferred writeback errors. Administrators running Linux-based NFS servers should assess affected systems and apply the relevant security update.

articles.headline: Linux AMDGPU VCE prevents partial address patches
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update prevents partial address patches in the AMDGPU VCE driver, tracked as CVE-2026-53375. Administrators running Linux systems with affected AMD graphics hardware should review exposure and deploy the relevant kernel update.

articles.headline: Linux GRE changes now require network administration privileges
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update requires CAP_NET_ADMIN in the device network namespace for GRE changelink operations, tracked as CVE-2026-63829. Administrators using containers or network namespaces should patch affected systems to prevent unauthorized tunnel changes.

articles.headline: Linux fbdev fixes a use-after-free flaw in mode handling
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update fixes a use-after-free vulnerability in the fbdev store_modes function, tracked as CVE-2026-63826. Administrators should patch affected kernels because memory-safety flaws can cause crashes or enable further exploitation.

articles.headline: Linux NTFS3 blocks userspace writes to reserved extended attributes
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update makes NTFS3 reject direct userspace writes to reserved $LX extended attributes, tracked as CVE-2026-63833. Administrators using NTFS volumes on Linux should update affected systems to prevent unsafe modification of filesystem metadata.

articles.headline: Linux KVM removes a guest-triggerable kernel crash condition
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update replaces a guest-triggerable BUG_ON condition in KVM ioeventfd handling, tracked as CVE-2026-63806. Administrators hosting virtual machines should assess affected kernels because a guest may be able to trigger a host failure.

articles.headline: Linux kernel adds an upper bound check for AMD KFD node counts
articles.summary: A Linux kernel update adds an upper bound check for AMD KFD node counts tracked as CVE-2026-53376. Administrators running Linux systems with supported AMD GPUs should review affected versions and apply the relevant security update.

articles.headline: MSRC publishes CVE-2026-53374 for AMDGPU GART allocation
articles.summary: MSRC published information about the Linux AMDGPU driver failing to zero-initialize a GART table during allocation. Administrators managing affected Linux systems with AMD graphics should assess risk and apply applicable updates.

articles.headline: MSRC publishes CVE-2026-53392 for NFSv4 flexfiles validation
articles.summary: MSRC published information about NFSv4 flexfiles accepting a zero filehandle version count. Administrators using affected Linux NFS configurations should review exposure and apply applicable vendor updates.

articles.headline: MSRC publishes CVE-2026-63810 for Linux block-device handling
articles.summary: MSRC published information about Linux block-device code mounting the bdev pseudo-filesystem in userspace. Administrators should identify affected systems and apply applicable kernel or vendor updates.

articles.headline: MSRC publishes CVE-2026-53386 for the TI ADS1298 driver
articles.summary: MSRC published information about a missing bounds check in the Linux TI ADS1298 ADC driver's gain settings. Administrators running affected Linux devices should assess exposure and install applicable vendor updates.

articles.headline: MSRC publishes CVE-2026-63815 for Linux F2FS inline attributes
articles.summary: MSRC published information about an F2FS flaw involving unbounded inline extended-attribute sizes on certain inodes. Administrators should review affected Linux systems and apply applicable vendor updates when available.

articles.headline: User-assigned identities simplify shared Microsoft 365 automation
articles.summary: User-assigned managed identities can authenticate Microsoft 365 automation across multiple Azure resources and automation accounts. Administrators gain reusable identity management without tying credentials to the lifecycle of one automation account.

articles.headline: Azure Virtual Desktop Hybrid brings VDI to any hypervisor
articles.summary: Microsoft is previewing Azure Virtual Desktop Hybrid for running VDI workloads on Azure Arc-enabled Windows machines across third-party hypervisors. IT administrators can reuse existing infrastructure while managing supported Windows 11 and Windows Server hosts through Azure.

articles.headline: Intune Custom Compliance May Reduce the Eight-Hour Evaluation Delay
articles.summary: Intune custom compliance policies may support faster evaluations instead of making administrators wait up to eight hours for refreshed results. Shorter delays could help security teams detect and remediate noncompliant endpoints sooner.

articles.headline: Squid cache digest flaw can corrupt memory
articles.summary: CVE-2026-50012 causes memory corruption while Squid handles cache digest replies. Administrators operating affected proxy or caching infrastructure should prioritize vendor updates because memory corruption can cause crashes or potentially more severe compromise.

articles.headline: Squid FTP gateway flaw can expose memory contents
articles.summary: CVE-2026-47729 allows memory disclosure through Squid's FTP gateway. Administrators using Squid for FTP proxying should evaluate affected versions and apply vendor guidance to reduce the risk of sensitive data exposure.

articles.headline: CoreDNS rewrite plugin flaw enables remote denial of service
articles.summary: CVE-2026-62299 triggers a nil-pointer panic when the CoreDNS rewrite plugin processes a downstream response without an EDNS0 OPT record. Administrators should review affected deployments because remote requests could disrupt DNS availability.

articles.headline: CoreDNS flaw lets one 28-byte packet trigger remote denial of service
articles.summary: CVE-2026-62309 causes the CoreDNS proxyproto plugin to panic when it receives a crafted Proxy Protocol v2 datagram using a non-UDP transport. Administrators running affected DNS infrastructure should assess exposure and prioritize available updates or mitigations.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes Chromium use-after-free vulnerability CVE-2026-15905
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has inherited Chromium's fix for a use-after-free vulnerability in the Aura interface framework. Administrators should prioritize Edge updates to reduce the risk of browser-based exploitation.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium Ozone
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15904, a use-after-free vulnerability in Ozone. Administrators should deploy the latest Edge security update to reduce browser exploitation risk.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes out-of-bounds access flaw in Chromium V8
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15903, an out-of-bounds read and write vulnerability in V8. Administrators should prioritize the latest Edge update because memory corruption in the JavaScript engine can expose users to browser-based attacks.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium Cast
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15902, a use-after-free vulnerability in Cast functionality. Administrators should apply the latest Edge security update, especially on devices where browser casting is available.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium networking
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15901, a use-after-free vulnerability in network handling. Administrators should update Edge across managed devices to reduce exposure from malicious or compromised web services.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium GPU code
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15900, a use-after-free vulnerability in GPU processing. Administrators should deploy the latest Edge update to protect endpoints from malicious graphics content that could trigger memory corruption.

articles.headline: Microsoft Edge fixes use-after-free flaw in Chromium CameraCapture
articles.summary: Microsoft Edge has ingested Chromium changes that address CVE-2026-15899, a use-after-free vulnerability in CameraCapture. Administrators should update managed browsers to limit exposure when users interact with camera-enabled web content.

articles.headline: Copilot Analytics adds Agent 365 activity dashboard for managers
articles.summary: Managers will gain access to current and historical Agent 365 activity through the Copilot Analytics Agent Dashboard. The August 2026 release will help organizations monitor adoption and understand the use of centrally registered agents.

articles.headline: Purview raises daily auto-labeling capacity to 500,000 files
articles.summary: Microsoft Purview will increase auto-labeling capacity for SharePoint and OneDrive from 100,000 to 500,000 files per tenant each day. Administrators can protect existing data faster, reduce sensitivity-labeling gaps, and better prepare content for Microsoft 365 Copilot.

articles.headline: Outlook highlights all-day scheduling conflicts in meeting invitations
articles.summary: Outlook on the web and Windows will make conflicts with out-of-office periods and busy all-day events easier to identify. The change may reduce scheduling mistakes but has limited relevance to security administration.

articles.headline: Teams adds external call routing for GCC High and DoD users
articles.summary: Teams users in GCC High and DoD environments will be able to route all external calls to voicemail or their unanswered-call settings. Administrators should review calling policies before the September 2026 rollout, although the change has limited security impact.

articles.headline: Microsoft 365 Copilot connects Agent 365 agents through Agent2Agent
articles.summary: Agents published to Agent 365 will work as connected agents in Agent Builder and Copilot Studio when they implement the Agent2Agent protocol. Administrators can centrally manage reusable agents through the Microsoft 365 admin center ahead of general availability in August 2026.

articles.headline: Microsoft Maps New Trust-Based Attacks Across Software, Identity, and AI
articles.summary: Microsoft outlines how attackers exploit trusted packages, build pipelines, identities, tools, and AI agents to reach enterprise systems. IT administrators should review access controls and monitoring across software supply chains, cloud services, and AI workloads.

articles.headline: Microsoft adds acknowledgements for DHCP Server vulnerability CVE-2026-56159
articles.summary: Microsoft updated the advisory for the DHCP Server remote code execution vulnerability with acknowledgements only. The change adds no new technical details or remediation guidance, so administrators do not need to alter existing patching plans.

Intune Fixes the 60-Minute Autopilot Delay for Required Apps
articles.summary: Intune Management Extension 1.103.101.0 appears to remove the 60-minute delay that could affect required app installations after Autopilot enrollment. Administrators should verify the new behavior in their environment because it can shorten device provisioning and reduce post-enrollment support issues.

articles.headline: The Demise of the OWA Light Client
articles.summary: Microsoft will retire the lightweight OWA Light client in Exchange Server SE with an August 2026 update, requiring on-premises users to switch to the full OWA client. Available since Exchange 2007 for older browsers and alternative platforms without JavaScript, OWA Light affects only on-premises Exchange environments, not cloud-only users.

articles.headline: ACR Stealer: Two observed intrusion chains amid increased threat activity
articles.summary: Microsoft Defender Experts has observed increased ACR Stealer activity using ClickFix lures to trick employees into running malicious commands that steal browser passwords, session tokens, and sensitive documents, potentially enabling access to cloud resources and further intrusion. IT teams should monitor for ClickFix lures, suspicious WebDAV activity, and obfuscated PowerShell execution.

articles.headline: Microsoft Teams: Explicit recording consent for PSTN participants now available in GCC High and DoD
Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365): Session and Response Sharing in M365 Copilot
Microsoft schedules Copilot Cowork and Credits management AMA
articles.summary: Microsoft will host a live session on Copilot Cowork adoption, governance, scaling, and credit allocation. Administrators can use the guidance to improve usage visibility and control consumption-based AI costs.

articles.headline: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
articles.summary: AI agents plan and chain actions across systems without human approval at every step, so overly broad role assignments can grant access far beyond what was intended. Microsoft recommends treating each agent as a separate identity with a dedicated managed identity, least-privilege RBAC, a clearly defined scope, and secure tool binding before broad deployment.

articles.headline: Renewed as Microsoft MVP for Intune and Windows 365
AutoAcceptSsoPermission: The Continue to Sign In Prompt Fix
articles.summary: The AutoAcceptSsoPermission policy closes the gap between Windows user-consent requirements and managed-device onboarding by allowing administrators to preapprove the “Continue to sign in” prompt that appears during sign-in. Rudy Ooms demonstrates how the setting works on Intune-managed devices.

articles.headline: CVE-2026-59831 GitHub CLI `gh codespace jupyter` could allow remote code execution when connecting to a malicious Codespace
Admin control for SSO prompts in Windows; finally, an off switch!
articles.summary: In the EEA, Windows must now ask users before reusing their sign-in for other Microsoft apps, adding noise in managed environments where SSO is already configured. Microsoft has published a new registry control that lets administrators disable the prompt, available with the July 2026 update for Windows 11 24H2 (KB5101650).

articles.headline: How to Schedule Teams Meetings from Shared Mailboxes
articles.summary: Despite Microsoft documenting it as supported, uncertainty remains over whether Teams meeting invitations can be sent from a shared mailbox. It works when the delegate has full mailbox access and a Teams license, but currently only in classic Outlook, and the shared mailbox itself does not need a separate license.

articles.headline: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
articles.summary: On July 14, Microsoft Threat Intelligence uncovered a coordinated supply-chain compromise of the @asyncapi organization on npm, with five package versions republished using the same malicious loader. Because @asyncapi/specs is a transitive dependency and the code runs at import time rather than through postinstall, affected developer machines, CI/CD pipelines, and production services are not protected by “npm install --ignore-scripts.”

articles.headline: Why identity is the Copilot unlock
articles.summary: Microsoft FastTrack argues that stalled Copilot deployments are rarely a licensing or Copilot problem, but an identity problem because Microsoft 365 Copilot authenticates through Entra ID and inherits each user’s Graph access. Part two targets Microsoft 365 administrators and CIOs whose immature identity foundations have prevented users from accessing Copilot and left them without a control plane for future agents.

Turning threat intelligence into decisive action with Defender Experts
Microsoft Open-Sources an AI Workflow for Building Sentinel ASIM Parsers
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source, AI-guided workflow that creates, validates, and packages Microsoft Sentinel ASIM parsers. Security teams can normalize new data sources faster, improving the portability of detections, hunting queries, and analytics across vendor logs.

articles.summary: AdminDroid shows how to detect high-risk AI agents in Entra and block their access in real time using Identity Protection risk signals. Because a compromised or manipulated agent, including one affected by prompt injection, can operate autonomously across Microsoft 365 until detected, live risk-based Conditional Access is a better option than blocking all agents.

articles.summary: Exchange Online is gaining two features: a check that prevents duplicate dynamic distribution groups and cross-tenant message recall. Administrators must explicitly choose which external tenants may recall messages delivered to their organization and should assess the governance implications before enabling the feature.

Inside the Improved on-demand sync for Windows devices
articles.summary: Rudy Ooms explains how Intune’s improved on-demand sync can now wake sleeping Windows devices, preventing manual sync requests from having no effect while a device is asleep. This enables faster, more reliable deployment of policies and apps to devices in the field.

articles.headline: Hunting Local AI Tools on macOS with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
Passkeys by default and retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice authentication – A guide to stay calm
articles.summary: Jan Bakker provides a practical timeline for the retirement of Microsoft-provided SMS and voice MFA and the shift to passkeys by default. The first milestone is September 1, 2026, when all SMS and voice users will be automatically enabled and prompted to register a passkey at sign-in, so administrators should ensure everyone has a phishing-resistant method before the summer holidays reduce preparation time.

Manage Folders and Rules for Microsoft 365 Groups in Outlook
articles.summary: This practical guide explains how owners and members can create folders and inbox rules for Microsoft 365 group mailboxes in Outlook. Group mailboxes contain only Inbox and Deleted Items by default, so the feature can help teams organize growing email volumes, although it has limited relevance for most IT administrators.

articles.headline: Passkeys Become the Default as Microsoft Entra Retires SMS and Voice Authentication
articles.summary: AdminDroid summarizes Microsoft’s transition to a passkey-first Entra ID model and explains why phone-based MFA poses a growing risk as organizations increase their use of AI. SMS and voice codes travel over telephone networks and are vulnerable to interception, social engineering, and SIM swapping, making the article useful background for prioritizing passkey deployment.

articles.headline: Microsoft to Stop Providing Telephony-Based Authentication Methods for MFA in February 2027
articles.summary: Microsoft says in MC1426371 that it will stop providing SMS one-time codes and voice calls for MFA on February 1, 2027, and move all tenants toward passkeys as a second factor. Customers that still require SMS or voice must purchase the service from a telecommunications provider through Microsoft Security Store starting September 18, 2026, so administrators should begin deploying passkeys now to prevent users from losing their sign-in method.

articles.headline: Microsoft are set to retire SMS & Voice authentication methods
Defending SaaS-based applications against ShinyHunters OAuth abuse
articles.summary: Microsoft tracked campaigns from 2025 to 2026 in which actors linked to ShinyHunters abused trusted OAuth connections, vishing, and misconfigured guest access to reach SaaS applications such as Salesforce. A single entry point can quickly lead to large-scale CRM data extraction while bypassing standard sign-in alerts, so administrators should monitor OAuth-connected apps, review consent grants, and tighten guest access.

articles.headline: Improving Windows Search Box, with less clutter and more control
articles.summary: Microsoft is rolling out Windows search box improvements to Insiders in the Experimental Channel, including a cleaner home screen, clearer labels showing whether results come from apps, settings, files, or the web, and no advertising in web results. A new Privacy & security setting controls whether web and Microsoft Store suggestions appear, and endpoint administrators should watch how these controls eventually reach managed Windows clients.

How to Identify Obsolete SharePoint Online Sites with PowerShell
articles.summary: Tony Redmond demonstrates how to use PowerShell to identify obsolete SharePoint Online sites in large tenants, warning that background processes make LastModifiedDateTime unreliable and prone to false positives. Administrators cleaning up thousands of sites must combine multiple activity sources to determine which sites are truly inactive.

The Intune MDM Device Certificate KSP Renewal Bug: Why the Bit4id Provider Caused it
articles.summary: Rudy Ooms investigates why Intune MDM device certificates stopped renewing on some Windows 11 23H2 machines and identifies Bit4id’s Key Storage Provider as the cause. Because an unrenewed certificate can eventually cause the device to lose trust and break MDM communication, Intune administrators should check which KSP affected certificates use to prevent devices from silently dropping out.

articles.summary: Microsoft has added two tenant parameters to the Set-CsTenantFederationConfiguration PowerShell cmdlet to enforce external-access rules throughout the lifecycle of federated Teams group chats. Administrators should enable the controls so updated policies apply to existing chats, preventing external users from retaining access or being added through a third-party tenant when policy no longer permits it.

A recall succeeds only when the recipient tenant has explicitly allowed it, giving admins a new way to limit damage while keeping control with the recipient.

Windows 365 Frontline was renamed to Windows 365 Flex
articles.summary: Microsoft has renamed Windows 365 Frontline to Windows 365 Flex, with the rebranding now rolling out across Intune, including changing the device model value from “CloudPC Frontline” to “Windows 365 Flex.” Admins should update assignment filters, dynamic Entra group rules, and device category scripts to prevent devices from dropping out of policies.

articles.headline: Microsoft Releases Windows Admin Center 2606 with Security Fixes
articles.summary: Microsoft made Windows Admin Center 2606 generally available in Administration Mode and updated its installer to build 2.7.4. The release improves reliability and accessibility while correcting elevation-of-privilege and remote-code-execution issues, so administrators should prioritize upgrading management hosts.

It is useful for demos, backup testing, and Graph development when static CDX tenants provide stale, uninteresting data.

GigaWiper: Anatomy of a destructive backdoor assembled from multiple malware
articles.summary: Microsoft Threat Intelligence has analyzed GigaWiper, a Golang-based backdoor that combines several destructive malware families in one implant, from physical disk wiping to fake ransomware whose encryption keys are never stored. Threat actors select the destruction method through on-demand commands, making attacks more flexible and difficult to predict, and security teams should review the Defender detections and indicators of compromise in the report.

articles.headline: Hands-On Microsoft 365 Pulse – Weekly Updates (Week 27)
articles.summary: This week’s HANDS ON tek Pulse covers three SharePoint updates: SharePoint links in Teams now open in the SharePoint app, a new button web part arriving in late July can trigger predefined Copilot prompts or Power Automate flows, and admins can classify sites into custom groups in Catalog Management. It is useful for daily SharePoint administrators but is primarily a weekly roundup.

articles.headline: Autopilot failing with 0x81036501: Expected MDM URI was not found
articles.summary: Rudy Ooms of Call4Cloud examines incident IT1420224, in which Autopilot devices in tenants on ASU 0102 failed to enroll in Intune and returned error 0x81036501, “Expected MDM URI was not found.” The post explains what triggers the MDM URI error and what caused the incident for endpoint admins troubleshooting stalled enrollments.

articles.headline: How to Disable the Print Spooler Service on Domain Controllers
articles.summary: AdminDroid explains why the Print Spooler service should be disabled on domain controllers and outlines several ways to do so. The service is enabled by default and gives attackers a direct path to remote code execution and privilege escalation on critical servers, making its removal a recommended part of Active Directory hardening.

Years of oversharing can become searchable by any licensed user, so admins should establish these controls before rollout rather than afterward.

articles.summary: Tony Redmond updates his guide to reporting managers and direct reports from Entra ID, replacing the retired Azure AD modules with Microsoft Graph. E5 customers already have the required tools in Entra ID, Conditional Access, Intune, and Defender, but must configure and scope them deliberately before scaling because one compromised or unmanaged account can expose the entire Microsoft 365 data estate through the user’s full permissions.

Unlike Reset this PC, it requires neither USB media nor a separate image, simplifying recovery of locked or damaged endpoints.

articles.headline: Intune EAM Now Automatically Updates Managed Applications
articles.summary: Microsoft has made Intune Enterprise Application Management auto-updates generally available for incremental application releases. IT administrators can reduce manual packaging, version drift, and exposure to known vulnerabilities across managed endpoints.

articles.headline: 5 insights from Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar™ for Cloud Security Posture Management
articles.summary: Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar for Cloud Security Posture Management forecasts that the CSPM market will grow from $2.82 billion to $6.96 billion by 2030 and places Microsoft among the leaders. It highlights a shift from periodic compliance to continuous, risk-based governance within CNAPP platforms, but is primarily market and positioning material rather than an actionable change for admins.

articles.headline: First look at the SharePoint API usage report
articles.summary: A new SharePoint API Usage report is available in Microsoft Graph beta, showing how apps use SharePoint APIs in a tenant. It is disabled by default and must be enabled through the new report settings with the ReportSettings.ReadWrite.All permission, although the endpoints do not yet enforce the documented permissions and a global admin can enable it regardless.

articles.headline: Turning Azure Policy Signals into Actionable Governance Insights
A Deep Dive into Controlled Feature Rollout: Why Features are turned off by default
articles.summary: Rudy Ooms examines Controlled Feature Rollout, the mechanism Microsoft uses to release new Windows features and hotfixes gradually and disabled by default. The post explains how an individual device determines when it receives a feature, helping admins understand why features appear at different times across their fleet.

articles.headline: KB – employeeLeaveDateTime show empty (null)
articles.summary: When the employeeLeaveDateTime attribute appears as null in Entra ID, the usual cause is missing permissions because this sensitive attribute requires User-LifeCycleInfo.Read.All for access through Microsoft Graph. The value becomes visible after consent is granted, which is important for admins building lifecycle or offboarding automation around departure dates.

articles.headline: Why Windows is the hardest passkey surface in 2026 and what Entra admins should expect
articles.summary: Data from large passkey deployments shows that Windows has the lowest sign-in success rate of any major platform in 2026, with Windows users struggling where iPhone users sign in seamlessly despite using the same identity provider and policy. Entra admins planning passkey deployments should expect their Windows fleet to generate the most support requests and pilot-test it especially thoroughly.

articles.headline: Purview Data Loss Prevention Introduces File Quarantine
articles.summary: Purview DLP is adding a “move to quarantine” action that isolates files violating DLP policies in a secure SharePoint location instead of merely blocking them, replacing the original with a tombstone text file that explains what happened and why. The feature is currently in preview and is scheduled for general availability in early June 2026 (MC1288527).

It is useful for Intune admins who want to prevent EPM rules from breaking after the next vendor update or elevating unintended files.

Team membership effectively grants broad access to sensitive content, making this useful for legal recordkeeping requirements, while membership changes can also be tracked in the audit log.

Other updates include curated organizational prompts in Copilot Chat, AI editing suggestions in Copilot Pages, more voice styles in Microsoft 365 Copilot, and usage-based billing for the Work IQ APIs.

articles.summary: This detailed technical guide explains how Win32 apps work in Intune, including what an .intunewin file contains, how IntuneWinAppUtil packages it, and how it is uploaded to the service. It also shows how Win32 apps can support scripting and registry changes and provide an alternative to remediations for organizations without an Enterprise license, making it a useful reference for admins deploying RMM, EDR, and other agent-based apps.

articles.headline: Prevent Sensitive Data Leakage Across Unmanaged Apps with Purview and Entra
articles.summary: Microsoft is launching Network Data Security, which extends Purview DLP to the network layer through Entra and detects sensitive data in motion, including text pasted into AI chatbots, files uploaded to personal cloud storage, and traffic sent to unauthorized SaaS apps beyond the reach of Microsoft 365’s built-in controls. It gives IT admins real-time visibility and the ability to block leaks that previously might only have been discovered afterward.

articles.headline: Hands-On Microsoft 365 Pulse – Weekly Updates (Week 26)
articles.summary: The month’s first Pulse summarizes June updates across SharePoint, Teams, and Copilot, with the retirement of Remote Event Receivers in SharePoint Online being the most important change for admins because dependent custom solutions must now plan their transition. Custom domains can also be excluded from Copilot web grounding, while a new Recap app in Teams brings all meeting recaps together in one place.

articles.headline: Mass convert groups from AD to Entra ID using PowerShell
articles.summary: Entra’s new Group Source of Authority conversion allows synchronized AD groups to move to Entra ID management without losing their identity, membership, or access. Marius Solbakken provides a PowerShell script for bulk conversion with error handling and logging, saving admins from manually processing hundreds of groups during a cloud migration.

articles.summary: The removeAllAccessForUser Graph API, which Teams documentation recommends for removing external chats containing malicious content, does not remove the user from the chat as its name suggests and only partially hides the messages. Admins relying on it for content moderation should know that it provides much weaker control than expected.

He also discusses Microsoft’s voluntary severance package taking effect and expresses concern about the expertise being lost as many veterans leave; for IT admins, this is primarily a reference and reading update rather than a technical change.

articles.headline: Autopilot 0x800705b4: App Control Blocks the Intune Management Extension
articles.summary: An Autopilot provisioning process stalls at “Preparing your device for mobile management” and ends with error 0x800705b4 because an App Control policy (WDAC), rather than Autopilot itself, blocks the Intune Management Extension from running. Admins deploying App Control should explicitly allow IME or device provisioning will fail.

articles.headline: Announcing Microsoft 365 for IT Pros (2027 Edition)
articles.summary: Tony Redmond’s “Microsoft 365 for IT Pros” book series is now in its thirteenth edition, with the 2027 content split so that Purview and Power Platform have their own books. The 330-page Purview book covers data lifecycle management, eDiscovery, DLP, information protection, and compliance, but it is a commercial Gumroad e-book rather than Microsoft product news.
